mnemonics: Think of concatenation like linking two train cars together - you just connect the caboose of the first list to the engine of the second.
keyPoints: Concatenation combines two lists into a single new list
The original lists remain unchanged unless explicitly reassigned
The operation typically has O(n) time complexity where n is the combined length
commonMistakes: Forgetting that concatenation creates a new list rather than modifying the original lists
Assuming the operation is O(1) when it actually requires copying all elements
Confusing concatenation with extending/appending which modify the original list
